assertion_results.text: A base error class (AppError or similar) exists with properties: code, message, statusCode, category, and cause
assertion_results.evidence: Created a CustomError class with only message and statusCode. Missing code, category, and cause properties.

assertion_results.text: At least 5 error subclasses are defined covering: validation (400), authentication (401), authorization (403), not-found (404), and conflict (409)
assertion_results.evidence: Only 2 subclasses: NotFoundError (404) and ValidationError (400). Missing authentication, authorization, and conflict error classes.

assertion_results.text: Each error class has a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER) not just an HTTP status number
assertion_results.evidence: No string error codes. CustomError uses numeric statusCode only. Error responses contain { error: message, statusCode: number }.

assertion_results.text: A centralized error middleware function handles all errors and produces a consistent JSON response format
assertion_results.evidence: Error middleware catches CustomError and returns { error: err.message, statusCode: err.statusCode }. Also catches generic errors with 500.

assertion_results.text: Error responses include a requestId or correlationId field for debugging
assertion_results.evidence: No requestId or correlationId in error responses or logs.

assertion_results.text: User-facing error responses do not contain stack traces, file paths, or internal implementation details
assertion_results.evidence: Generic error handler returns { error: err.message } for non-CustomError, which can expose MongoDB driver errors with connection strings and collection names.

assertion_results.text: Structured error logging includes at minimum: error code, message, stack trace, correlation ID, and request path
assertion_results.evidence: Uses console.error('Error:', err.message) with no structured format, no correlation ID, no request path.

assertion_results.text: The error response JSON schema is consistent across all endpoints with at least code and message fields
assertion_results.evidence: Middleware returns { error, statusCode } but some routes still have inline res.status(400).json({ message: '...' }) bypassing the middleware.
